ogTitle: Warrumbungle vs Warwick Farm: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Warrumbungle and Warwick Farm.
heading: Warrumbungle vs Warwick Farm — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Warrumbungle, New South Wales and Warwick Farm, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Warrumbungle has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Warwick Farm (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Warrumbungle or Warwick Farm safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Warrumbungle has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Warwick Farm scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Warrumbungle scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Warwick Farm scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Warrumbungle or Warwick Farm?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Warrumbungle has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
